2606.20898.title: The Token Tax of Epistemic Accuracy: Comparing RAG and Long-Context Architectures for Document-Grounded Generative AI Applications
2606.20898.paper_summary: Document-grounded assistants built on large language models are increasingly used in high-stakes, knowledge-intensive work. Their usefulness, however, may depend on how evidence is allocated before generation. We investigate such a claim by comparing two grounding architectures: (a) retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) that retrieves a few relevant passages, and (b) long-context prompting, which loads the whole document collection in context. We view these as two regimes of "epistemic access" on an accuracy--cost frontier. We use "epistemic accuracy" to capture model correctness that depends on having the right evidence. We posit that broader access (via long context) can increase it, but with a "token tax" (i.e., a substantial increase in cost due to larger input token consumption). We probe this framing with a case study in manufacturing safety training. Using an expert-validated benchmark, we evaluate 972 answers across three machines, two small language models, and three retrieval/in-context prompting approaches. Long-context prompting achieved the highest correctness (73.1% vs. 65.4% for semantic RAG), but at 26 times the per-query token cost. We interpret this gap as the token tax of broader evidentiary access. We carefully discuss the implications of our findings for resource-constrained organizations.
2606.20898.paper_summary_zh: 基於大型語言模型的文件基礎助手在高風險、知識密集的工作中越來越受到使用。 然而，它們的有效性可能取決於生成前證據的分配方式。 我們通過比較兩種基礎架構來調查這一主張：(a) 檢索增強生成（RAG），它檢索幾個相關段落，以及 (b) 長上下文提示，該方法將整個文檔集合加載到上下文中。 我們將這兩者視為“認識論訪問”在準確性—成本邊界上的兩種模式。 我們使用“認識論準確性”來捕捉模型正確性，這取決於擁有正確的證據。 我們假設更廣泛的訪問（通過長上下文）可以提高準確性，但會產生“令牌稅”（即，由於更大的輸入令牌消耗而導致成本的顯著增加）。 我們通過製造安全培訓的案例研究來探討這一框架。 使用專家驗證的基準，我們評估了三台機器、兩個小型語言模型和三種檢索/上下文提示方法的972個答案。 長上下文提示達到了最高的正確性（73.1% 對比語義 RAG 的 65.4%），但每查詢的令牌成本為26倍。 我們將這一差距解釋為更廣泛證據訪問的令牌稅。 我們仔細討論了我們的發現對資源有限的組織的影響。
2606.20898.author: Austin Hamilton et.al.
2606.20898.authors: Austin Hamilton, Ryan Singh, Michael Wise, Ibrahim Yousif, Arthur Carvalho, Zhe Shan, Mohammad Mayyas, Lora A. Cavuoto, Fadel M. Megahed
